primary_factors: Most concrete hourly forecast details show the wintry mix focused on Sunday pre-dawn, followed by a strong warm-up Sunday—this usually allows roads to recover well before Monday bus time in Kent County.

Main Monday risk is not snow depth but patchy black ice/refreeze on bridges, overpasses, shaded neighborhood streets and rural connectors; this more often supports a delay/extra caution than a full closure unless it becomes widespread.

Winter Storm Watch for counties just north of Kent through Monday evening creates meaningful track/thermal-profile uncertainty (tail risk of sleet/freezing rain shifting into Kent during the Monday AM commute).

Power is a wildcard: ~52k Consumers Energy outages statewide with confirmed Kent County impact (Rockford-specific concentration unknown). If outages persist or expand Sunday night, closures become more likely.

News/decision signals are quiet: no neighboring district closures visible yet and FOX 17 shows no active closings/delays; no community buzz—this usually correlates with ‘open’ unless conditions deteriorate Sunday night.
decision_rationale:
Cross-referencing the experts: Meteorology, History, News, and Infrastructure are broadly aligned that—based on the only detailed hourly data provided—the impactful wintry mix is Sunday morning and Sunday warms well above freezing, which typically reduces Monday closure odds in West Michigan. Safety’s ‘Mon 04:30–09:00 worst window’ is acknowledged as a Michigan-typical refreeze/black-ice heuristic rather than something directly supported by a Monday-specific hourly forecast in the dataset; infrastructure agrees the likely Monday problem is patchy slick spots, not plows being overwhelmed. News intel shows no pre-call closure signals and no neighboring-district domino effect yet, which keeps probability from rising into the 50%+ range.

What could make this forecast wrong is the watch-driven uncertainty: if the storm track/thermal profile shifts south into Kent and produces ongoing sleet/freezing rain or accumulating snow during 6–8 AM Monday, Michigan districts often close. Separately, if Kent County outages persist/expand into Monday morning (schools or key traffic corridors without power), that can force closure even if roads are mostly passable. Given these tail risks but a more-likely ‘wet/bare main roads + isolated slick spots’ outcome, the Brier-optimized call is low-to-moderate: 30%.

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 2 round(s) with 11.0% spread. Average probability: 29%.
